headline: Assault, attempted murder, assassination: The rogue Freemasons going on trial in Paris
description: A coach, a race car driver, a trade unionist: Nothing seemed to connect these otherwise unremarkable individuals. Yet all were targeted by the same criminal organization. At its head were prominent members of a Masonic lodge in a Paris suburb. These 'brothers,' now enemies, are standing trial from March 30.

headline: Ireland gives Trump a lesson on Europe
G7 leaders call for stabilizing energy markets amid Middle East war
France sentences Chinese captain of suspected Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker to one year in jail
description: Chen Zhangjie was sentenced in absentia after the French navy boarded the Boracay tanker in September, before releasing the vessel and its crew days later.

headline: Flavel, a victim of police violence, tells his story: 'If there hadn't been videos, no one would ever have believed me'
description: On March 16, the former musician went out to order food in Noisiel, in the eastern Paris suburbs. Police violently arrested him, as seen in a video widely shared online. He described to Le Monde the events that led him to file a complaint with the internal affairs agency.

headline: Is it ever a good idea to... wear leather pants?
description: In fashion, as in life, it is important to know the lines you should not cross. Or do so deliberately.

headline: 'StravaLeaks': How Le Monde located 18,000 French military personnel with a fitness app
description: For more than two years, Le Monde has been tracking a significant proportion of France's military personnel on Strava, a popular fitness app, on almost a daily basis. Their movements can be monitored, including in sensitive areas, putting both military operations and the servicemembers involved in them at risk.

headline: French court dismisses Cardiff's €120 million claim against Nantes over footballer Sala's death
Air Canada CEO announces retirement after row over English-only condolence message
Several new far-right mayors remove EU flags from French town halls
Adrien Bilal, winner of the 2026 Best Young Economist Award: 'Climate change will cost 50% of GDP by 2100'
Israel braces for delayed economic fallout of war in Iran
French AI firm Mistral borrows $830 million to build Paris data center
UN force in Lebanon says soldier killed in country's south
In wartime Tehran, rare photos capture daily life under bombardment
Emmanuel Grégoire takes office as the new Paris mayor
Migrant Channel crossings: France-UK negotiations over border funding get bogged down
EU Pact on Migration and Asylum: France lags on transposition, raising fears of legal confusion
US reopens its embassy in Venezuela after seven years
Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.

headline: Escaping Putin's Russia: The father and daughter persecuted over a drawing
description: Alexei Moskalyov and his daughter, Maria, are symbolic victims of the Kremlin's repression. The father was jailed after posting an anti-war drawing his teenage daughter made at school. They have just arrived in France and are under humanitarian protection.

headline: 'No Kings' protests against Trump focus on war in Iran: 'The US is on the wrong side of history'
description: Millions turned out to protests across the US against the Trump administration, with the movement's epicenter located in the twin cities of Minneapolis-Saint Paul, after two Americans were killed by federal agents there in January.

headline: Iranians are caught in the trap of Trump's incoherence
'Trump's vociferations no longer reassure financial markets'
'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
